name: Pastrana and Zorita de los Canes Castle Loop
name_original: Unión de los Ríos Tajo y Guadiela — circular por el Castillo de Zorita de los Canes
description: Cycle a moderate 37.4-mile (60.2 km) route along the Tajo and Guadiela rivers, passing the historic Castle of Zorita de los Canes.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Pastrana traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills and river valleys, often featuring views of reservoirs and historical sites. The region's terrain includes moderate climbs and descents, with some routes venturing into more challenging, elevated areas. This area offers a mix of open countryside and routes that pass through small towns. The elevation changes provide varied cycling experiences across the local topography. Best road cycling routes around Pastrana The most popular road cycling route is Almoguera and Sierra de Altomira Loop, a 49.8 miles (80.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 46 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the Sierra de Altomira. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Pastrana and Zorita de los Canes Castle Loop, a moderate 37.4 miles (60.2 km) path. This route offers a mix of open roads and passes by historical landmarks such as Zorita de los Canes Castle. Local road cyclists also love the Buendía Dam – Entrepeñas Reservoir Dam loop from Almonacid de Zorita, a 59.4 miles (95.6 km) trail leading through reservoir landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 17 minutes. Road cycling around Pastrana is defined by rolling hills, reservoir views, and historical village passages. The network offers options for different ability levels. The routes in Pastrana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a small number of reviews. More than 160 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Pastrana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Pastrana?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes documented around Pastrana, offering a variety of experiences across the region's rolling hills and river valleys.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Pastrana?
A: While the routes around Pastrana are generally categorized as moderate to difficult due to the rolling hills and elevation changes, some moderate options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014660694/pastrana-and-zorita-de-los-canes-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pastrana and Zorita de los Canes Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer a good introduction to the area's terrain without extreme challenges. It's a 37.4-mile (60.2 km) path with a mix of open roads and historical sights.

Q: What kind of challenging road cycling routes can I find near Pastrana?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014687264/almoguera-and-sierra-de-altomira-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almoguera and Sierra de Altomira Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain through the Sierra de Altomira, covering 49.8 miles (80.1 km). Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buendía Dam – Entrepeñas Reservoir Dam loop from Almonacid de Zorita&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 59.4 miles (95.6 km) through reservoir landscapes.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling options in the Pastrana area?
A: Yes, for longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buendía Dam – Entrepeñas Reservoir Dam loop from Almonacid de Zorita&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 59.4 miles (95.6 km) route, typically completed in about 4 hours 17 minutes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014687264/almoguera-and-sierra-de-altomira-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almoguera and Sierra de Altomira Loop&lt;/a&gt; also offers a considerable distance at 49.8 miles (80.1 km).

Q: What historical landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass by significant historical sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014660694/pastrana-and-zorita-de-los-canes-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pastrana and Zorita de los Canes Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3527044" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zorita de los Canes Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Other routes may offer views or access to sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3527077" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Recópolis Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5406352" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almoguera Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that feature reservoirs or water views?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buendía Dam – Entrepeñas Reservoir Dam loop from Almonacid de Zorita&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, leading through beautiful reservoir landscapes, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3906128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buendía Dam&lt;/a&gt;. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14890765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolarque Dam loop from Almonacid de Zorita&lt;/a&gt;, which also offers scenic water views.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling around Pastrana?
A: The region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling, with milder temperatures and pleasant scenery. Summers can be hot, especially in open areas, while winters are cooler but often still suitable for cycling on clear days, though some higher elevation routes might be less comfortable.

Q: What do other cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Pastrana?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, which includes rolling hills and reservoir views, as well as the opportunity to pass through charming small towns and historical sites.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic highlights along the routes?
A: Beyond the historical castles and archaeological sites, the routes offer numerous scenic viewpoints, particularly those traversing the Sierra de Altomira or passing by the large reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2782935" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Road Along the Tajuña River&lt;/a&gt; is also noted for its picturesque setting.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Pastrana?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Pastrana are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014660694/pastrana-and-zorita-de-los-canes-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pastrana and Zorita de los Canes Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14890765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolarque Dam loop from Almonacid de Zorita&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Pastrana?
A: While many routes feature moderate to difficult terrain with significant elevation changes, families with older children or those comfortable with longer distances and some climbs might enjoy the moderate routes. It's advisable to check the specific elevation profiles and distances to ensure they match your family's cycling ability.

Q: Where can I park when starting a road cycling route in Pastrana?
A: Parking is generally available in the towns and villages where routes typically begin, such as Pastrana itself or Almonacid de Zorita. Look for public parking areas within these communities, often near the town centers or main access points.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or food along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass through small towns and villages where you can find local cafes, bars, or restaurants to refuel. It's always a good idea to plan your stops in advance, especially on longer routes, as services might be sparse in more remote sections of the countryside.
tips: This is one of the meanders of the Guadiela River where the Bolarque Yacht Club is located. Space where boats are located in a beautiful space!
The castle of Zorita de los Canes stands on a hill on one of the banks of the Tagus River. It is a fortification of Arab origin built in the 9th century.
Nice area with a castle ruin in the town and Visigothic ruins nearby.

In the plain of the Arles river, between the Tagus and the Tajuña, the Villa Ducal de Pastrana welcomes us. Its multiple samples of artistic and cultural heritage have given it the title of Artistic Historical Site.

Pastrana is enriched with figures and literary stories; Thus, its Carmelite convents were founded by Saint Teresa and Saint John of the Cross. Moratín later settled in the town for long periods of time and, closer to our days, Camilo José Cela immortalized it by portraying it in his Viaje a la Alcarria.

The Plaza de la Hora is governed by the imposing Ducal Palace, in Renaissance style and with an abundance of Mannerist elements. Some wonderful coffered ceilings also designed by Alonso de Covarrubias, in Plateresque style, have been preserved. Likewise, the skirting boards of Toledo tiles in the Mudejar style stand out.
It offers its visitors the opportunity to visit various museums and emblematic monuments of the town.
DUCAL PALACE
TAPESTRY MUSEUM
SANTA TERESA V CENTENARY MUSEUM

faqs.question: What is the estimated cycling time for this route?
faqs.answer: The estimated cycling time for the "Union of the Tajo and Guadiela Rivers — circular via the Castle of Zorita de los Canes" route is approximately 3 hours and 11 minutes. This duration is for a racebike and can vary based on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What are the road conditions like for road cycling?
faqs.answer: While the region offers many cycling opportunities, specific details for this exact route's surface are not extensively detailed. It's likely to involve a mix of paved roads and potentially unpaved or gravel sections, especially near riverbanks or natural areas. Road cyclists should verify the exact route's surface to ensure suitability for their bikes, as some segments in the broader Tajo area can be unpaved and difficult.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is rated as 'moderate' in difficulty, with an elevation gain of nearly 900 meters over 60 kilometers. While some sections might be easy, others could involve significant elevation changes or challenging surfaces. It's best suited for cyclists with good fitness and some experience, rather than absolute beginners, especially given the potential for varied terrain.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect significant natural beauty and historical landmarks. The route offers views of the Tajo and Guadiela river valleys, featuring diverse landscapes including mountains, cliffs, pine and juniper forests, riparian forests, farmlands, and olive groves. The area is part of the Alto Tajo Nature Park, known for its rich river ecosystem.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or viewpoints will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The most notable landmark is the historically significant Zorita de los Canes Castle, an Arab fortification from the 9th century, offering stunning views of the Tagus River. You'll also pass by the Club Náutico Bolarque, the Tagus River in Zorita de los Canes, and the settlement of Pastrana.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region experiences varied weather. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. Always check the local forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Where can I park for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Specific parking facilities for this exact circular route are not detailed. However, if starting in Zorita de los Canes, you would likely find parking options within or near the village, especially close to the castle or other local amenities. It's advisable to check local maps or resources for designated parking areas before your visit.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other paths. Notably, it covers about 16% of the Camino Natural del Tajo and 12% of the Camino de Santiago en Guadalajara. Smaller portions also overlap with Senda del convento, Calle Damas, Senda de Valdemartin, Camino del Valle, and Senda Fuenperemnal.

faqs.question: Is the route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the natural areas are generally accessible, specific rules for dogs on this exact cycling route are not provided. If the route includes public roads, dogs are typically allowed. However, if it enters protected natural areas or private land, specific leash rules or restrictions might apply. Always keep your dog under control and be mindful of local regulations and wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees?
faqs.answer: Generally, no specific permits or entrance fees are required for cycling on public roads or established trails in this region of Spain. However, if you plan to visit specific attractions like the Zorita de los Canes Castle, there might be an entrance fee for the monument itself. Always check the latest information for any protected areas you might pass through.

faqs.question: Are there places to refuel or find amenities along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through or near settlements like Zorita de los Canes and Pastrana, where you would likely find cafes, restaurants, or small shops to refuel and access amenities. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for longer cycling stretches between villages.
